stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 audited results and possible final dividend scheduled for board meeting on May 12, 2026 (6 days away). Q4FY26 earnings call same day at 17:30 IST. Q3 FY26 was a sharp miss: PAT fell ~60% YoY to Rs 40.57 Cr; EPS Rs 37.00 vs estimate Rs 56.52 (-34.54% surprise). Stock has experienced ~33% drawdown over past 30 days pre-results. Company has secured projects from a large innovator client moving operations out of China.
stocks.sector_context: API/CDMO part of Nifty Pharma at 52-week high. Indian CDMOs benefiting from China+1 sourcing — Neuland, Syngene, Divi's seeing surge in RFQs. PLI scheme of Rs 6,940 cr supporting domestic API. India API market growing at 7.74% CAGR to USD 22.18 bn by 2031. Headwind: 15-20% surge in fermentation input costs in early 2025. However, sector dispersion is wide and Neuland is currently underperforming peers.
stocks.peer_comparison: Among CDMO leaders (Lupin, IPCA, Divi's, Natco) that beat estimates last quarter while Aurobindo, Zydus, Biocon lagged. Neuland doubled CMS revenue in earlier prints but recent Q3 miss has caused steep underperformance vs peers. 30-day drawdown materially worse than Nifty Pharma which is positive MoM.
red_flags: Q3 FY26 PAT crashed ~60% YoY and missed estimates by -34.5%
30-day stock drawdown of ~33% indicates institutional de-risking pre-results
Binary event risk: May 12 results could deepen drawdown if Q4 misses
CDMO project lumpiness can create uneven earnings prints
catalysts: Q4 FY26 + FY26 audited results on May 12, 2026 — high-impact binary event
Possible final dividend recommendation on May 12
FY27 guidance and CDMO order book commentary on the call
China+1 supply chain wins pipeline conversion

stocks.recent_news: FY26 consolidated PAT up 41.8% YoY to Rs 747.93 cr; revenue up 38% to Rs 7,002.16 cr. Q4FY26 revenue up 29.1% YoY to Rs 2,119.21 cr; PBT up 27% to Rs 410.85 cr. Final dividend of Rs 6.70/share recommended. MoU with Kalyani Strategic Systems on Mar 5, 2026 for indigenous naval and unmanned platforms. Antique raised target post-Q4 surprise. CMP Rs 2,871.70 (May 5).
stocks.sector_context: Indian warship building in structural multi-decade upcycle driven by naval modernisation pipeline targeting 200+ ship fleet. Strong order visibility across surface combatants, submarines, next-gen platforms. Mazagon Dock CNC negotiations completed for Rs 99,000 cr P-75(I) program — sector-wide positive sentiment with Mazagon up 18% in 2 sessions.
stocks.peer_comparison: L1 in next-gen corvette order worth Rs 33,000 cr (finalization expected Q1 FY27). L1 contender in Rs 70,000 cr P-17B Frigate program. Ashika Buy with Rs 2,730 target — already breached. Future order pipeline Rs 1.8 lakh cr. P/E 42.55 — premium but in line with defence cohort like Mazagon Dock and Cochin Shipyard.
red_flags: Trading near 52-week high (Rs 3,538.40) at Rs 2,871.70 with elevated P/E of 42.55
Concentration risk on lumpy government defence orders and timing of conversion
Some broker targets (Ashika Rs 2,730) already breached — limited upside if no fresh order news
catalysts: Next-gen corvette order finalization in Q1 FY27 (Rs 33,000 cr; GRSE is L1)
P-17B Frigate program L1 outcome (Rs 70,000 cr)
Final dividend of Rs 6.70 per share
Kalyani Strategic Systems MoU execution on indigenous naval/unmanned platforms

stocks.recent_news: Titagarh Naval Systems subsidiary received in-principle approval from DG Shipping for Brownfield Expansion at Falta, West Bengal under government Shipbuilding Development Scheme — Rs 6.1 bn capex with Rs 1.29 bn government capital assistance. Received LoA from Banaras Locomotive Works for WAG-9HC shell assembly worth Rs 91.12 cr (deliverable by Aug 31, 2026). Jefferies initiated Buy with Rs 810 target. CMP Rs 800 (May 5).
stocks.sector_context: Indian rail rolling stock sector benefiting from strong passenger and metro coach demand; structural shift from freight to passenger trains in line with Indian Railways priorities. Vande Bharat program supports order pipeline. Rail capex theme remains a multi-year story.
stocks.peer_comparison: Jefferies Buy target Rs 810 vs CMP Rs 800 — limited upside to broker target. PRS contributes 77% of order book. Among preferred picks in rail rolling stock alongside BEML and others. Faces competition for Vande Bharat orders from BEML, Alstom JVs, etc.
red_flags: Stock at Rs 800 essentially at Jefferies Rs 810 target — limited near-term upside
Vande Bharat execution risk: first 16-car body completion only by end-FY26, first fully-assembled train Q3 FY27
Capital intensive shipyard expansion adds execution risk
catalysts: Vande Bharat sleeper trains JV delivery ramp-up in Q3 FY27
Brownfield shipyard expansion at Falta — defence/shipbuilding diversification
Rs 91.12 cr WAG-9HC order execution by Aug 31, 2026
Q4 FY26 results (calendar pending)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 4, 2026: Consolidated PAT up 30.6% YoY to Rs 1,129.16 cr (+19.5% QoQ); net sales record Rs 13,459.25 cr (+10.2% YoY). Total lending portfolio crossed Rs 2 lakh cr (32% YoY growth) to Rs 2,07,368 cr. Disbursements +28% YoY to Rs 24,947 cr; ROA up 63 bps YoY to 2.07%. Borrowing limit raised to Rs 2 lakh cr. Stock up ~5% post-results to Rs 361.95 intraday; closed Rs 348-362 on May 4-5.
stocks.sector_context: Indian NBFC sector strong; AMC and life/health insurance verticals showing margin expansion. Life insurance VNB margin up 260 bps YoY to 20.6%; health insurance combined ratio improved from 105% to 103% YoY. Diversified financial conglomerate model in favor.
stocks.peer_comparison: Stock returned 75.76% over past year vs Sensex -4.02% (alpha ~80 pp). Brokerages retain Buy with up to 24% upside post-Q4. P/E 25.2; mcap Rs 94,994.7 cr. Asset quality improving: NBFC GS2/GS3 down 38 bps QoQ to 2.42%; HFC down 19 bps QoQ to 0.76%. Outperforming Bajaj Finance and most NBFC peers on TSR.
red_flags: Marketsmojo flags 'margin pressure concerns' despite headline profit growth
Valuation grade rated 'EXPENSIVE' as of May 2026 after strong run
Stock at Rs 362 only ~2% below 52-week high of Rs 369.25 — limited cushion
catalysts: Continued lending portfolio growth post Rs 2 lakh cr milestone
Insurance margin expansion runway in life and health
Higher borrowing limit (Rs 2 lakh cr) supports continued AUM scale-up
Brokerage target upgrades (up to 24% upside per BS)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: PAT up 10.9% YoY to Rs 126 cr; revenue up 11% to Rs 395.2 cr; record EBITDA of Rs 183.66 cr (margin 46.5%) up 14.9% YoY. Final dividend of Rs 4/share recommended. AGM scheduled July 7, 2026. Non-MF revenue surged 24% YoY. Stock jumped up to 9% intraday post results to ~Rs 790; CMP Rs 803 on May 6, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian MF industry AUM crossed Rs 55 lakh cr in Dec 2025 (~18% YoY growth). RTA duopoly: CAMS dominant 68% market share, KFin Tech 31%. Industry tailwind from continuing retail SIP inflows. Non-MF (KRA, account aggregator, insurance repo) emerging as growth lever.
stocks.peer_comparison: KFin Technologies delivered 182% one-year returns vs CAMS 76% — KFin outperforming on growth and now marginally higher market cap (Rs 255 bn vs CAMS Rs 236 bn). KFin services 24 of 43 AMCs vs CAMS 18 of 46 by count, but CAMS retains larger AUM-weighted share. KFin growing revenue faster on a smaller base.
red_flags: Modest 10.9% PAT growth lags faster-growing peer KFin Tech
Market share concentration in MF RTA — limited diversification despite 24% non-MF growth
Peer KFin Tech gaining ground on revenue growth and stock performance
catalysts: Final dividend of Rs 4/share — record date pending AGM approval
AGM on July 7, 2026
Non-MF revenue scale-up (24% YoY surge) provides diversification angle
Continued MF industry AUM growth at ~18% YoY
